_meta.description: Tool definitions for the Solace Performance Test Harness. Pass the 'tools' array to the Claude API tools parameter, or use individually with any OpenAI-compatible tool-calling interface.
_meta.prerequisites: Ansible installed; sdkperf_c binary in pubSubTools/; config/credentials.yaml present (copy from config/credentials.yaml.example). All commands must run from the repository root on a Linux filesystem (not a Windows path — use a /tmp clone if needed).
_meta.working_directory: Repository root (scripts use BASH_SOURCE-relative paths and must be invoked with ./ from within the repo)

tools.name: list_benchmarking_tests
tools.description:
List all available pre-configured fixed-target benchmarking testsets. Returns testset names that can be passed to run_benchmarking_test. Each testset targets a specific Solace broker tier and messaging configuration.

Command: ls benchmarking-tests/*.sh | sed 's|benchmarking-tests/||; s|\.sh||' | sort

tools.name: run_benchmarking_test
tools.description:
Run a pre-configured fixed-target benchmarking testset against a Solace broker. Each scenario is tested once at the expected peak rate and reported as PASS (consumer rate ≥ 95% of target) or FAIL. Results are written to results/{prefix}_{msg_type}_result.txt and analysed automatically on completion.

Tier naming: standard = Standard edition; ent-1k/10k/100k = Enterprise tiers (max ~1k/10k/100k connections); 3560-ADB4 = Solace 3560 hardware appliance with ADB4 blade.
Variants: direct = direct messaging; gm-ha/gm-noha = guaranteed messaging with/without HA; quick = abbreviated run covering key scenarios only.

Command: ./start-benchmarking-test.sh {test_name} {broker}

test_name.description: Name of the benchmarking testset to run.

tools.name: run_discovery_test
tools.description:
Discover the maximum sustained throughput of a Solace broker for each scenario using an exponential probe followed by binary search. No target rates are required — the harness finds the highest rate the broker can sustain end-to-end. Results are written to results/{prefix}_{msg_type}_result.txt and analysed automatically.

Algorithm: exponential probe starts at upper_bound/1024 and doubles until first failure; binary search converges to ±1% precision (or ±500 msgs/sec floor) within search_iterations (default 10) iterations.

Upper bounds: the probe starting point scales from upper_bound/1024, so set upper_bound higher for more capable brokers (e.g. 25000000 for the 3560 ADB4 hardware appliance).

Command: Sets sshuser and upper-bound env vars, then calls engine/run-binsearch-testset.sh with scenario arrays built from the provided sizes, fanouts, parallel_hosts, and msg_types.

prefix.description: Result file prefix. Result written to results/{prefix}_{msg_type}_result.txt.

sshuser.description: SSH user on test hosts. Defaults to the value in config/credentials.yaml.

parallel_hosts.description: Number of parallel publisher/subscriber host pairs to use. More hosts allow higher aggregate rates.

msg_sizes.description: Message sizes in bytes to include in the scenario matrix.

fanouts.description: Fanout values to include in the scenario matrix. Fanout is the number of consumer subscriptions per published message.

upper_bound_direct.description: Probe upper bound for direct messaging (msgs/sec). Conservative default suits software brokers. Increase for hardware appliances (e.g. 25000000 for 3560 ADB4).

upper_bound_nonpersistent.description: Probe upper bound for non-persistent messaging (msgs/sec).

upper_bound_persistent.description: Probe upper bound for persistent (guaranteed) messaging (msgs/sec).

tools.name: run_custom_testset
tools.description:
Run a previously generated custom discovery testset from custom-sets/. Custom testsets are created by start-custom-discovery-test.sh and saved as custom-sets/{name}.sh. Use list_results to see results from prior runs.

Command: custom-sets/{name}.sh [{broker}]

name.description: Custom testset name — the filename without path or .sh extension (e.g. 'my-broker-test' for custom-sets/my-broker-test.sh).

broker.description: Broker IP address or hostname. If omitted, uses the broker embedded in the testset at creation time (if any).

tools.name: run_single_test
tools.description:
Run a single performance test scenario directly via the Ansible playbook and return the aggregate publisher and consumer rates. Useful for spot-checking a specific scenario without running a full testset. Does not write a result file.

target_msg_rate is the expected total consumer-side rate and must include the fanout multiplier (e.g. 100k msgs/sec published with fanout=5 means target_msg_rate=500000). The playbook divides by parallel_hosts×fanout to derive the per-publisher rate.

Command: engine/run-test.sh -e '{"broker":"...","parallel_hosts":...,"target_msg_rate":...,"msg_size":...,"sdk_fanout":...,"runlength":...,"mt":"...","sshuser":"..."}'

fanout.description: Number of consumer subscriptions per published message.

target_msg_rate.description: Target total consumer message rate (msgs/sec), including the fanout multiplier. Example: 1000 msgs/sec published with fanout=5 → target_msg_rate=5000.

parallel_hosts.description: Number of parallel publisher/subscriber host pairs.

sshuser.description: SSH user on test hosts. Defaults to value in config/credentials.yaml.

tools.name: analyse_results
tools.description:
Parse result files and print a diagnostic summary table plus findings for each identified bottleneck or configuration issue.

Checks performed:
- 'Error in clock' entries → publisher CPU saturated, cannot sustain the requested rate
- Publish rate flat across all scenarios → publisher host or NIC is the absolute ceiling
- Low publish rate consistent across message sizes → WAN or throttled network link
- Consumer rate ~50%/33%/25% of expected → 1/2, 1/3, or 1/4 of publisher hosts reached (SSH issue)
- Publisher or consumer bandwidth near 1 GbE (>100 MB/s) or 10 GbE (>1 GB/s)
- Low persistent publish rate with target >> achieved → broker storage IOPS limit
- Consumer rate < publish_rate × fanout → message drops or subscription failures
- All persistent fail while direct passes → VPN spool quota, endpoint-create permission missing, or guaranteed messaging not licensed

Command: engine/analyse-result-set.sh [paths...]

paths.description: Result file paths, directories, or shorthand names to analyse. Shorthand names resolve to results/{name}_result.txt (e.g. '1k_mixed' → results/1k_mixed_result.txt). Omit to scan the entire results/ directory.

tools.name: list_results
tools.description:
List available result files in the results/ directory. Result filenames follow the pattern {testsetprefix}_{msg_type}_result.txt. Pass any of these names (or paths) to analyse_results for diagnostic analysis.

Command: find results/ -name '*_result.txt' | sort
